message:
Fix merge() function dropping nested fields (#5728)

Resolves TNZ-227

## Summary

Fix a bug where the `merge()` TQL function was dropping nested fields
instead of performing a recursive deep merge.

**Problem:** `merge({hw: {sn: "XYZ123"}}, {hw: {model: "foobar"}})`
produced `{hw: {model: "foobar"}}` instead of the expected deep merge
result `{hw: {sn: "XYZ123", model: "foobar"}}`.

**Root cause:** The old implementation used AST-level record spread
operators, which only perform shallow field-level merging at the record
boundary.

**Solution:** Replaced the AST spread approach with a data-level
recursive merge using the existing `tenzir::merge()` function from
`data.hpp`. This properly handles nested records at the data layer.

## Changes

- **libtenzir/builtins/functions/misc.cpp**: Rewrote the `merge()`
function to use data-level recursive merging instead of AST-level record
spread
- Added 4 additional includes required for the new implementation:
`arrow_table_slice.hpp`, `data.hpp`, and `view.hpp`
- The new implementation:
  - Handles null values correctly
  - Recursively merges nested records
- Preserves the right side value when merging non-record types (right
wins)
- Uses `policy::merge_lists::no` to merge records without merging lists

## Testing

Added comprehensive test cases:
- **merge.tql/merge.txt**: Issue reproduction - verifies that nested
record merge preserves all fields across nested boundaries
- **merge_deep.tql/merge_deep.txt**: Edge cases including:
  - 3-level deep nesting (`x.y.z`)
  - Type conflict handling (string vs nested record)
  - Disjoint records (no overlapping keys)

All 615 integration tests pass with these changes.

## Test Plan

- Run full integration test suite: `uvx tenzir-test --root test-legacy`
- Verify the specific test cases: `merge.tql` and `merge_deep.tql` pass
- Manual verification: `echo 'merge({hw: {sn: "XYZ123"}}, {hw: {model:
"foobar"}})' | tenzir`

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+---
+title: merge() function recursive deep merge for nested records
+type: bugfix
+authors:
+  - mavam
+  - claude
+pr: 5728
+created: 2026-02-05T15:56:10.953688Z
+---
+
+The `merge()` function now performs a recursive deep merge when merging two records. Previously, nested fields were dropped when merging, so `merge({hw: {sn: "XYZ123"}}, {hw: {model: "foobar"}})` would incorrectly produce `{hw: {model: "foobar"}}` instead of recursively merging the nested fields. The function now correctly produces `{hw: {sn: "XYZ123", model: "foobar"}}` by materializing both input records and performing a deep merge on them.

@@ -0,0 +1,6 @@
+from {
+  a: {hostname: "server1", domain: "acme.com", hw: {sn: "XYZ123"}},
+  b: {hw: {model: "foobar"}},
+}
+x = merge(a, b)
+this = x

@@ -0,0 +1,40 @@
+from {
+  a: {x: {y: {z: 1, w: 2}}},
+  b: {x: {y: {z: 99, v: 3}}},
+  c: {key: "string_value"},
+  d: {key: {nested: 1}},
+  e: {foo: 1},
+  f: {bar: 2},
+}
+deep = merge(a, b)
+conflict = merge(c, d)
+disjoint = merge(e, f)
+// Null handling: left null returns right, right null returns left.
+null_left = merge(null, {b: 2})
+null_right = merge({a: 1}, null)
+null_both = merge(null, null)
+// Null field within a record: right side overwrites null field.
+null_field = merge({a: 1, b: null}, {b: 2, c: 3})
+// Empty records.
+empty_left = merge({}, {a: 1})
+empty_right = merge({a: 1}, {})
+// 4+ levels of nesting to validate full recursion.
+deep4 = merge({a: {b: {c: {d: {e: 1, f: 2}}}}}, {a: {b: {c: {d: {e: 99, g: 3}}}}})
+// Scalar-to-scalar conflict: right side wins.
+scalar_conflict = merge({value: 1}, {value: 2})
+// List fields: right side replaces left (no list merging).
+list_conflict = merge({items: [1, 2]}, {items: [3, 4]})
+this = {
+  deep: deep,
+  conflict: conflict,
+  disjoint: disjoint,
+  null_left: null_left,
+  null_right: null_right,
+  null_both: null_both,
+  null_field: null_field,
+  empty_left: empty_left,
+  empty_right: empty_right,
+  deep4: deep4,
+  scalar_conflict: scalar_conflict,
+  list_conflict: list_conflict,
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+// Multi-row merge: exercises columnar if_else with mixed null/non-null parents.
+from {a: {x: 1, y: 2}, b: {x: 10, z: 3}},
+  {a: {x: 1, y: 2}, b: null},
+  {a: null, b: {x: 10, z: 3}}
+this = merge(a, b)
